title: mollia alsinifolia
intro: Mollia alsinifolia, also known as the bog currant or mountain currant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America, where it grows in wet, acidic environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. Mollia alsinifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are said to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
